name: Vegetarian Cassoulet
recipeIngredient: 4 large red potatoes, peeled, cut into fourths
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort and wash the garbanzo beans; place in a 6-quart Dutch oven. Cover with water 2 inches above the beans; let stand 8 hours or overnight. Drain.
2. Add 3 quarts of water, 1 tablespoon minced garlic, and bay leaf to the Dutch oven. Cook over medium-high heat for 2 hours or until beans are tender. Remove and discard the bay leaf. Set beans aside.
3. Melt butter or margarine in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add 1 tablespoon of garlic, mushrooms, and next 3 ingredients (dried thyme, dried rosemary, crushed, and dried oregano). Cook, stirring constantly, for 5 minutes.
4. Add white wine and tomato paste to the skillet; cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes.
5. Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon minced garlic, turnips, rutabaga, onions, carrots, salt, pepper, olive oil, and vegetable broth. Spread into an aluminum foil-lined roasting pan.
6. Bake at 500 degrees F for 20 minutes, stirring once.
7. Spoon roasted vegetables over beans in Dutch oven; pour broth over the vegetables. Sprinkle with dry breadcrumbs. Bake at 325 degrees F for 1 1/2 hours or until vegetables are tender.
